mnemonics.title: McCall's Quality Factors Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Remember as: CREEP UMT FR (Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Integrity, Portability, Usability, Maintainability, Testability, Flexibility, Reusability)

mnemonics.title: CMM Levels Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: I RAN D Q O - Initial, Repeatable, And, Defined, Quantitatively, Optimizing (First letters form a phrase!)

mnemonics.title: Fishbone 6M Categories
mnemonics.content: Mother's Pet Eats Fish - Man, Power (Machine), Procedures (Method), Materials, Environment, Feedback (Measurement)

mnemonics.title: ISO 9126 Characteristics
mnemonics.content: F R U E M P - Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability (First letters form a word!)

acronyms.usage: Process-oriented activities to prevent defects during development
Product-oriented activities to detect defects in the final product
Framework describing evolutionary improvement of software processes
Improved version of CMM with integrated disciplines
Company-wide approach to continuous quality improvement
Continuous improvement cycle developed by Deming
Six Sigma methodology for process improvement
Unit used to measure software size for defect density calculation
Six Sigma metric for measuring defect rates

visualTips.visualization: Picture a pyramid with 3 tiers: Product Operation (top - correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability), Product Revision (middle - maintainability, testability, flexibility), Product Transition (bottom - portability, reusability, interoperability)
Think of a staircase with 5 steps: 1-Initial (chaos), 2-Repeatable (basic management), 3-Defined (standardized), 4-Managed (measured), 5-Optimizing (continuously improving)

Draw a horizontal spine with a head labeled 'Effect' (the problem). Six bones branch off representing: Man (people), Machine (equipment), Method (process), Material (resources), Measurement (metrics), Environment (conditions)
Imagine a circle with 4 arrows: Plan (design the improvement), Do (implement it), Check (measure results), Act (standardize or start over). It's a continuous loop moving clockwise

analogies.analogy: QA is like preventive medicine (keeping you healthy), while QC is like diagnostic tests (finding problems after they exist)

Like learning to drive: Level 1 is learning randomly, Level 2 is following rules you remember, Level 3 is following a proper driving school curriculum, Level 4 uses a speedometer and fuel gauge, Level 5 continuously improves your driving technique
Like a report card for software - it provides grades on different subjects (quality factors) to show how well the software performs overall
Like aiming for a bullseye - traditional quality might hit the target 95% of the time, but Six Sigma aims to hit so precisely you'd miss only 3.4 times out of a million attempts
